scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a supported passenger service-status request only after PNR, ticket, DCS, station, service-log, and carrier-policy records aligned on the current state and next customer action.
Missing PNR, ticket, flight, bag tag, airway bill, and cargo booking references produced a blocked-state request for minimum prerequisites instead of guesswork.
The lane rejected discretionary compensation and policy override requests and escalated them as approval-bound exceptions.
Baggage custody gaps across acceptance, load, transfer, and arrival records were documented and routed to baggage services without inventing delivery or liability outcomes.
Customs hold and missing advance cargo data triggered refusal of release language and escalation to customs/compliance and cargo operations.
Ground delay and EDCT disruption handling preserved movement-impact communication while avoiding dispatch, load-control, warehouse-release, or safety authority claims.
Security screening override requests were refused and routed to TSA or airport security with safe customer-service language.
Wrong-lane aircraft swap and gate-control work became a clean handoff to dispatch or operations with required artifacts and communication constraints.
Repeat mishandled-baggage contacts and missed callbacks were escalated as complaint or service-recovery cases rather than recycled as routine status checks.
The lane preserved airport and air cargo terminology such as PNR, SSR, CRO, DCS, BSM, BRS, bag tag, PIR, airway bill, ULD, load plan, EDCT, and permit to unlade.
Conflicting public, carrier, airport, tenant, and station signals were recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.
Tenant-specific accessibility, baggage, and cargo rules were applied without discarding the public DOT, CBP, TSA, and IATA baseline, and assumptions were documented explicitly.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and transportation-and-logistics industry-profile abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and transportation-and-logistics contract.
The package stays inside airport customer-service resolution and does not claim dispatch, customs, security, maintenance-release, safety, legal, refund, compensation, or policy-exception authority.
